metadata.recent_signal: Launched KScan AI (Feb 2026) -- AI-powered MSME credit assessment; AI-Powered BFSI OS (Dec 2025); ISO 42001 AI cert (Jan 2026); Clari5 hiring Head of AI/ML Engineering
metadata.match_reason: Core product is OCR/document-AI for BFSI (bank statement, KYC, ITR, PAN, Aadhaar parsing) -- direct match to Amit's gov-doc OCR experience; recently launched KScan AI and GenAI BFSI platform.
metadata.cold_outreach_angle: On-premise OCR pipeline (DocTR + DocLink + Nemotron-70b, ~90% entity extraction on Aadhaar/PAN/GST) maps directly to Perfios Document AI core; production RAG at 50K queries/month shows ML at scale
metadata.notes: 18+ roles on Naukri; ISO 42001 AI mgmt certified; strongest fintech OCR/doc-AI overlap. Email pattern: firstname.lastinitial@perfios.com (confirmed nikita.b@perfios.com). 1K-5K employees. Clari5 is a Perfios subsidiary doing real-time fraud detection ML.

research_summary: Perfios is India's largest B2B fintech SaaS company (1K-5K employees, Bengaluru HQ) processing bank statements, KYC, OCR documents for BFSI. Feb 2026: launched KScan AI (AI for 30M MSME credit assessment). Dec 2025: AI-Powered BFSI Operating System. Jan 2026: ISO 42001 AI certification (first BFSI company in India). Clari5 (subsidiary) actively scaling ML team for real-time fraud detection. 7 contacts mapped: Director Engineering Data Science (IIT KGP), Senior Manager Engineering, 3 AI/ML ICs, 2 TA recruiters. Email pattern: firstname.lastinitial@perfios.com.
jd_full_text:
Data Scientist - Time Series & Sequence Modeling (most relevant active Perfios listing on Naukri, Apr 2026; original AI/ML Engineer - Document AI listing has expired)

Must-Have:
- Strong background in Machine Learning, Deep Learning, and Statistical Modeling.
- Proficiency in Python and ML libraries (TensorFlow, PyTorch, Scikit-learn, XGBoost).
- Hands-on experience with time-series data, anomaly detection, or fraud detection.
- Strong feature engineering skills, especially with high-dimensional and noisy behavioral data.
- Knowledge of data processing frameworks (Spark, Kafka, Flink, etc.) for streaming/real-time data.
- Experience deploying models into production systems (ML Ops, APIs, containerized environments).

Role: Machine Learning Engineer
Industry: BPM/BPO / FinTech
Department: Data Science & Analytics
Key skills: TensorFlow, PyTorch, Time Series, Spark, Kafka, Sequence Modeling, Machine Learning
Location: Bengaluru / Mumbai / New Delhi
Experience: 3-5 years
Salary: 15-25 Lacs PA

Document AI context: Perfios core platform parses Aadhaar, PAN, bank statements, KYC, ITR for BFSI. KScan AI (Feb 2026) uses AI to assess 30M Indian MSMEs. ISO 42001 certified. Clari5 subsidiary does real-time ML fraud detection for banks.
tailoring_notes: 1. Professional Summary: rewrote to lead with 'document AI, OCR pipelines, and ML systems' instead of generic RAG framing; added explicit mention of ~90% entity extraction on Aadhaar/PAN/GST and TensorFlow/PyTorch/Docker/AWS stack to mirror JD language. 2. Technical Skills reordering: promoted Python/ML frameworks (TensorFlow, PyTorch, Scikit-learn) to line 1; added a dedicated 'Document AI and OCR' category as line 2 (DocTR, DocLink, Tesseract, on-premise PDF pipelines, entity extraction) since that is Perfios's core product; moved Hardware/IoT bullet out and added Kafka to Infrastructure (explicitly required in JD for streaming ML). 3. Work Experience -- Silver Touch: moved the OCR pipeline bullet to position 1 (from position 4) because Aadhaar/PAN/GST entity extraction is the strongest match to Perfios Document AI; added 'zero cloud API calls, all on-premise' to highlight data sovereignty which Perfios cares about (ISO 42001, privacy-first messaging). 4. Projects and Certifications unchanged. Two-page layout maintained.

contacts.research_hook: Posted on LinkedIn recruiting Computer Vision engineers for his 'highly enthusiastic team of Data Scientists and challenging Computer Vision projects' -- used a custom CV test built by his team to fast-track screening. IIT Kharagpur alumni. He is the hiring manager for the Data Science group and is actively growing the team.
contacts.subject: your CV hiring post and an OCR engineer
contacts.content:
Hi Anupam,

Saw your LinkedIn post looking for CV engineers to join the data science team -- the custom screening test your team built was a nice touch.

I worked on a production OCR pipeline at Silver Touch for a client with strict data sovereignty requirements: all on-premise, 200+ page PDFs, entity extraction on Aadhaar, PAN, and GST numbers. The approach combined DocTR and DocLink (each did something the other missed), then used Nemotron-70b on a 250GB client server to merge outputs -- ended up at roughly 90% entity extraction accuracy. Before that I spent 5 months at IIT Delhi doing a systematic architecture search across 1,000+ CNN/BiLSTM configurations for a wearable gesture recognition system, so the model evaluation side is familiar territory too.

I am 2 years in on production AI and have been following what Perfios is building in document AI and the KScan AI launch. If your team is hiring on the ML side, I would love 15 minutes to hear how you think about the CV and document parsing work.

Amit
github.com/amit-gohel | amitgohel.dev | linkedin.com/in/amitgohel-ai
contacts.follow_up_1_subject: re: your CV hiring post and an OCR engineer
contacts.follow_up_1_content:
Hi Anupam,

Bumping this in case it got buried. Since I sent that note, I looked more closely at the KScan AI release -- the MSME profile analysis from unstructured documents is a hard problem, and the on-prem constraint I dealt with at Silver Touch (Aadhaar/PAN data never leaving the building) maps to exactly that kind of sensitivity.

Happy to close the loop if now is not the right time.

Amit
amitgohel.dev | linkedin.com/in/amitgohel-ai
contacts.follow_up_2_subject: re: your CV hiring post and an OCR engineer
contacts.follow_up_2_content:
Hi Anupam,

Last note from me. If Perfios is not actively hiring on the ML/document AI side right now, totally understood -- would still appreciate being on your radar for when it comes up.

Amit
amitgohel.dev | linkedin.com/in/amitgohel-ai

contacts.research_hook: Senior Manager Engineering at Perfios since 2018. Perfios just launched KScan AI (Feb 2026) -- AI that analyzes 30M Indian MSME profiles for credit and risk signals -- and the AI-Powered BFSI OS (Dec 2025). Both products require production ML engineering at scale, which is Bharat's domain.
contacts.subject: KScan AI launch and a document AI engineer
contacts.content:
Hi Bharat,

Went through Perfios's KScan AI release last month -- extracting credit and risk signals from 30M MSME profiles is a serious document parsing problem, and the ISO 42001 certification signals the kind of governance-first AI approach that makes on-prem pipelines matter.

Quick context on me: 2 years in production AI. At Silver Touch I built an on-premise OCR pipeline for sensitive government documents -- Aadhaar, PAN, GST numbers from 200+ page PDFs, using a hybrid DocTR + DocLink approach merged by an on-prem Nemotron-70b model, hitting roughly 90% entity extraction accuracy. Before that I rebuilt RAG infrastructure serving 50,000+ queries/month, migrating from FAISS to Milvus and improving MRR from 45% to 95% after benchmarking 6 embedding models.

If Perfios is hiring on the ML/document AI side, would value 15 minutes of your time to understand where the team is focused.

Amit
github.com/amit-gohel | amitgohel.dev | linkedin.com/in/amitgohel-ai
contacts.follow_up_1_subject: re: KScan AI launch and a document AI engineer
contacts.follow_up_1_content:
Hi Bharat,

Following up in case the earlier note slipped through. One thing I should have mentioned: the OCR work at Silver Touch had a hard no-cloud constraint -- the client purchased a 250GB server specifically so Aadhaar and PAN data never left the building. That setup (on-prem GPU, self-hosted LLM, zero external API calls) seems directly relevant to how Perfios positions KScan AI for BFSI data sensitivity.

Happy to step back if timing is off.

Amit
amitgohel.dev | linkedin.com/in/amitgohel-ai
contacts.follow_up_2_subject: re: KScan AI launch and a document AI engineer
contacts.follow_up_2_content:
Hi Bharat,

Last note. If the team is not actively hiring right now, no worries at all -- good luck with the KScan AI rollout, it looks like a strong product.

Amit

contacts.research_hook: His headline says 'Data Scientist @ Perfios | NLP | Custom Fine-Tuned Models | Document AI' -- he works on the exact Document AI stack Amit has production experience in. Amit built an on-premise OCR pipeline achieving ~90% entity extraction on Aadhaar/PAN/GST from 200-page PDFs using DocTR + DocLink + Nemotron-70b. Strong peer-to-peer referral candidate.
contacts.subject: working on document AI at Perfios
contacts.content:
Hi Sahil,

Came across your profile while looking into Perfios -- your work on NLP and Document AI is exactly the area I have been building in.

At Silver Touch I built an on-premise pipeline for sensitive government documents: combined DocTR and DocLink (each had different failure modes on layout vs. character accuracy), then used Nemotron-70b on the client's own server to merge the outputs. Ended up at roughly 90% entity extraction accuracy on Aadhaar, PAN, and GST numbers from 200+ page PDFs, with zero cloud API calls. I also fine-tuned embedding models and ran retrieval benchmarks (FAISS vs. Milvus vs. Qdrant) for a RAG system serving 50,000+ queries/month.

I am 2 years in on this kind of work and looking at roles where document parsing and NLP are the core problem. If Perfios is hiring on the ML side, would it make sense to connect? Even a quick note on how the team thinks about this would be helpful.

Amit
github.com/amit-gohel | amitgohel.dev | linkedin.com/in/amitgohel-ai
contacts.follow_up_1_subject: re: working on document AI at Perfios
contacts.follow_up_1_content:
Hi Sahil,

Following up on this. One thing I have been thinking about since: the hardest part of the DocTR + DocLink merge was deciding when to trust each engine on a per-region basis -- layout blocks, tables, and dense text each had different reliability profiles. Curious whether your team runs into similar trade-offs with custom fine-tuned models at scale.

No pressure if timing is off.

Amit

contacts.research_hook: SDE 2 building AI/Gen AI features at Perfios in Bengaluru. Uses Kafka (streaming ML pipelines, which the JD explicitly requires) and FastAPI/Docker. Ex-Naukri SSE who moved into AI engineering -- similar trajectory to Amit's shift from general dev to production AI.
contacts.subject: AI engineering at Perfios -- quick question
contacts.content:
Hi Souvik,

Quick one -- saw you are building AI/Gen AI features at Perfios. I noticed your stack overlaps a fair bit with mine: FastAPI, Kafka, Docker, Python, and the Gen AI side.

I have spent the past 2 years on production AI work: permission-aware agentic RAG chatbot serving 500+ concurrent users on LangChain/LangGraph, a multi-model form generation pipeline (GPT-4o + Qwen3-Coder + GPT-4o-mini, 200+ forms in month 1), and an on-premise OCR pipeline for Aadhaar/PAN/GST document parsing achieving roughly 90% extraction accuracy. All production, all serving real users.

I have been looking at Perfios's document AI work and I think there is a real fit. If the team is hiring and you know who handles ML/AI hiring, would you be open to pointing me in the right direction?

Amit
